name: Introduction to Business & Economics
description: A broad introduction to business concepts and economic thinking — how firms are organized, how markets work, basic financial literacy, entrepreneurship, and the global economy.

modules.title: What Is Business?
modules.objectives: Identify the three main types of business ownership (sole proprietorship, partnership, corporation); describe business goals and the roles of key stakeholders.
modules.readingNote: Investopedia — What Is a Business? Definition, Types, and How to Start One
modules.videoNote: Crash Course Economics — Business Types and Goals
modules.assignment: Compare three real businesses (one sole proprietorship, one partnership, one corporation). For each, identify at least two stakeholders, explain how the ownership structure affects decision-making, and list one advantage and one disadvantage of the structure.

modules.title: Economics Basics
modules.objectives: Define scarcity, choice, and opportunity cost; explain how these concepts drive economic decision-making; interpret a basic supply and demand graph.
modules.readingNote: Investopedia — Scarcity: Definition, Causes, and Economic Impact
modules.videoNote: Crash Course Economics #1 — Introduction to Economics
modules.video2Note: Crash Course Economics #4 — Supply and Demand
modules.assignment: Choose a personal decision you have made recently (e.g., how to spend a Saturday afternoon). Identify the opportunity cost. Then find a real-world example of scarcity in the news and explain how it affects supply and demand.

modules.title: How Markets Work
modules.objectives: Explain the price mechanism and market equilibrium; differentiate types of competition; describe basic consumer behavior.
modules.readingNote: Investopedia — Market Economy: Definition, How It Works, and Characteristics
modules.videoNote: Crash Course Economics #7 — Market Failures, Taxes, and Subsidies
modules.video2Note: TED-Ed — How Does the Stock Market Work?
modules.assignment: Select a product of your choice. Draw a supply-and-demand diagram for it, then describe what would happen to the equilibrium price if consumer incomes rose by 20%. Identify which type of competition (perfect, monopolistic, oligopoly, monopoly) best describes that market and justify your choice.

modules.title: Business Functions
modules.objectives: Describe the four core business functions (operations, marketing, finance, HR); explain how these departments collaborate to achieve organizational goals.
modules.readingNote: Investopedia — Business Plan: What It Is, What It Includes, and How to Write One
modules.videoNote: Crash Course Business — Soft Skills #1: Organizational Structure
modules.video2Note: TED-Ed — What Makes a Good Manager?
modules.assignment: Pick a well-known company (e.g., Nike or Apple). For each of the four core functions, describe one decision that company might make and explain how that decision connects to the other functions. Present your findings in a one-page written summary or a diagram.

modules.title: Entrepreneurship
modules.objectives: Define entrepreneurship and identify what entrepreneurs do; explain how to identify a market opportunity; weigh the risks and rewards of starting a business.
modules.readingNote: Investopedia — Entrepreneur: What It Means to Be One and How to Get Started
modules.videoNote: Crash Course Business — Entrepreneurship #1: What Is Entrepreneurship?
modules.video2Note: TED — How Great Leaders Inspire Action (Simon Sinek)
modules.assignment: Brainstorm a simple business idea that solves a problem you have noticed in your school or community. Identify the target customer, the key value you offer, two major risks, and one potential reward. Write a one-paragraph executive summary of your concept.

modules.title: Money & Banking
modules.objectives: Explain the three functions of money; distinguish checking from savings accounts; define interest and explain how banks operate.
modules.readingNote: Investopedia — Money: What It Is, How It Works, Types, and Importance
modules.videoNote: Crash Course Economics #11 — Money and Finance
modules.practiceNote: Khan Academy — Banking and Money Tutorial
modules.assignment: Compare two bank accounts offered by real banks: one checking and one savings. Record the interest rate, minimum balance, and one fee for each. Calculate how much interest $1,000 would earn in one year in the savings account using simple interest.

modules.title: Personal Finance
modules.objectives: Create a simple budget; distinguish income from expenses and needs from wants; set a short-term and long-term saving goal.
modules.readingNote: Investopedia — Personal Finance: Definition and Guide
modules.video2Note: TED-Ed — How to Build Good Financial Habits
modules.assignment: Build a monthly budget for a fictional 18-year-old with a part-time job earning $800/month. List all expenses, categorize each as a need or a want, and show whether the budget is balanced. Then set one short-term saving goal (under 6 months) and one long-term goal (1+ year).

modules.title: Business Finance
modules.objectives: Define revenue, costs, profit, and loss; calculate break-even point; read a simplified income statement.
modules.readingNote: Investopedia — Break-Even Analysis: Definition and How to Calculate
modules.videoNote: Crash Course Business — Finance #1: Revenue, Costs, and Profit
modules.video2Note: TED-Ed — How to Read an Income Statement
modules.assignment: A small bakery sells cupcakes for $3 each. Fixed costs are $600/month; variable cost is $1 per cupcake. Calculate the break-even quantity. Then construct a simple income statement for a month in which the bakery sells 500 cupcakes.

questions.question: Which type of business is owned and operated by a single person who is personally liable for all debts?
questions.explanation: A sole proprietorship has one owner who retains all profits but also bears unlimited personal liability for business debts.

questions.question: The concept that choosing one option means giving up the next best alternative is called:
questions.explanation: Opportunity cost is the value of the next-best alternative foregone when a choice is made.

questions.question: At market equilibrium, which of the following is true?
questions.explanation: Market equilibrium occurs at the price where quantity supplied equals quantity demanded, clearing the market.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "Which business function is primarily responsible for attracting and retaining employees" is: Human Resources.
questions.explanation: True. Human Resources is correct.

questions.question: An entrepreneur identifies an unmet customer need and builds a product to fill it. This is best described as:
questions.explanation: Recognizing an unmet need and creating a product or service to address it is the core of identifying a market opportunity.

questions.question: Which of the following is a function of money?
questions.explanation: Money's three functions are medium of exchange, unit of account, and store of value.

questions.question: If your monthly income is $1,200 and your monthly expenses are $950, your budget is:
questions.explanation: Surplus = income − expenses = $1,200 − $950 = $250.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "A company sells 400 units at $10 each and its total costs are $3,200. What is its profit" is: $400.
questions.explanation: False. The correct answer is: $800.

questions.question: Which of the 4 Ps describes where and how a product is made available to customers?
questions.explanation: Place (distribution) covers the channels through which products reach the customer, such as stores, websites, or direct delivery.

questions.question: A manager who makes all decisions alone and expects strict obedience from employees is using which style?
questions.explanation: An autocratic manager centralizes authority and makes decisions without input from subordinates.

questions.question: The break-even point is the level of sales at which:
options: Fixed costs are recovered and profit begins
questions.explanation: At break-even, total revenue = total costs, meaning the business has neither profit nor loss.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "Which account typically offers a higher interest rate — a checking account or a savings account" is: Savings account.
questions.explanation: True. Savings account is correct.

questions.question: Explain the difference between a need and a want. Give one example of each and explain how distinguishing between them helps with personal budgeting.
questions.answer: A need is something essential for survival or basic functioning (e.g., food, shelter, clothing). A want is something desirable but not essential (e.g., a new video game, designer shoes). Separating needs from wants helps a person prioritize spending, ensure essential expenses are covered first, and identify areas where discretionary spending can be reduced to increase savings.
questions.explanation: Needs vs. wants is a foundational personal finance concept that guides budgeting priorities.

questions.question: A student is deciding whether to spend Saturday studying for an exam or working a 4-hour shift at a café for $10/hour. Define opportunity cost and calculate the opportunity cost of choosing to study.
questions.answer: Opportunity cost is the value of the next best alternative foregone when a decision is made. If the student chooses to study, the opportunity cost is $40 (4 hours × $10/hour) in lost wages. The student must weigh this financial cost against the potential academic benefit of studying.
questions.explanation: Opportunity cost applies to time and resources, not just money.

questions.question: Identify two key risks and two key rewards of entrepreneurship. Use a real-world example to illustrate one of the risks.
questions.answer: Risks: (1) Financial loss — the entrepreneur may lose invested capital if the business fails (e.g., many restaurants fail within the first year, causing owners to lose their savings). (2) Uncertainty of income — unlike a salaried job, entrepreneurial income is unpredictable. Rewards: (1) Potential for high profits and wealth accumulation. (2) Independence and the ability to build something meaningful. Other valid answers accepted.
questions.explanation: Entrepreneurship involves trading job security for autonomy and the possibility of larger financial gains.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "Which form of business ownership provides the strongest protection of personal assets from business debts" is: Sole proprietorship.
questions.explanation: False. The correct answer is: Corporation.

questions.question: When the price of a good rises and quantity demanded falls, this illustrates:
questions.explanation: The law of demand states that, all else equal, quantity demanded decreases as price increases.

questions.question: A country produces wine more efficiently than any other good. According to comparative advantage, it should:
options: Import wine and export everything else
Specialize in wine production and trade for other goods
questions.answer: Specialize in wine production and trade for other goods
questions.explanation: Comparative advantage suggests countries gain from specializing in goods they produce at a relatively lower opportunity cost and trading for others.

questions.question: Which of the following best describes a multinational company?
options: A company that sells products only online
A company that operates in more than one country
A company that imports raw materials
questions.answer: A company that operates in more than one country
questions.explanation: A multinational company (MNC) has facilities, operations, or subsidiaries in multiple countries.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) refers to a company's obligation to:" is: Consider the impact of its decisions on all stakeholders and society.
questions.explanation: True. Consider the impact of its decisions on all stakeholders and society is correct.

questions.question: Digital transformation in a business context most accurately means:
options: Upgrading office computers every three years
Integrating digital technology into all areas of a business to change how it operates and delivers value
Moving a company's website to a new server
Replacing human workers entirely with robots
questions.answer: Integrating digital technology into all areas of a business to change how it operates and delivers value
questions.explanation: Digital transformation is a comprehensive shift in operations, culture, and customer experience driven by digital technology.

questions.question: A business plan's executive summary should:
options: Be the longest section of the plan
Provide a concise overview of the entire business concept
questions.answer: Provide a concise overview of the entire business concept
questions.explanation: The executive summary is a brief (1–2 page) overview that captures the mission, product, market, and financial highlights of the plan.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a fixed cost for a bakery?
options: Electricity used by the oven per hour
questions.explanation: Fixed costs remain constant regardless of output. Rent does not change based on how many loaves are baked.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "The 4 Ps of marketing include all of the following EXCEPT:" is: Product.
questions.explanation: False. The correct answer is: People.

questions.question: An exchange rate tells you:
options: The interest rate set by a central bank
How much of one currency is needed to buy one unit of another currency
The inflation rate in a country
The tariff rate on imported goods
questions.answer: How much of one currency is needed to buy one unit of another currency
questions.explanation: An exchange rate expresses the value of one currency relative to another (e.g., 1 USD = 7.2 CNY).

questions.question: Which financial statement shows a company's revenues and expenses over a specific time period?
questions.explanation: The income statement (also called the profit and loss statement) summarizes revenues, costs, and expenses over a reporting period.

questions.question: Automation in business is best described as:
options: Training employees to use new software
Using technology to perform tasks with minimal human intervention
Increasing the number of managers in a company
questions.answer: Using technology to perform tasks with minimal human intervention
questions.explanation: Automation uses machines, software, or AI to execute repetitive or complex tasks that previously required human effort.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "Scarcity in economics means that:" is: Resources are limited relative to human wants.
questions.explanation: True. Resources are limited relative to human wants is correct.

questions.question: A democratic management style is characterized by:
options: The manager making all decisions unilaterally
Employees making decisions without any manager input
The manager involving the team in decision-making while retaining final authority
An absence of any formal leadership
questions.answer: The manager involving the team in decision-making while retaining final authority
questions.explanation: Democratic managers consult their team, encourage input, and consider group opinions before making a final decision.

questions.question: Which of the following best explains why countries engage in international trade?
options: To take advantage of comparative advantages and access goods unavailable domestically
questions.answer: To take advantage of comparative advantages and access goods unavailable domestically
questions.explanation: Countries trade to benefit from specialization, lower costs, and access goods or resources they cannot efficiently produce.

questions.question: Simple interest on a $500 savings deposit at a 4% annual rate for 2 years equals:
questions.explanation: Simple interest = Principal × Rate × Time = $500 × 0.04 × 2 = $40.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "Which of the following is a primary goal of branding" is: To lower production costs.
questions.explanation: False. The correct answer is: To create a distinct identity and emotional connection with consumers.

questions.question: Describe two ways technology has changed a specific industry of your choice. Discuss one positive and one negative effect on workers in that industry.
questions.answer: Example (Retail): (1) E-commerce platforms allow retailers to reach global customers without physical stores, increasing revenue potential. (2) Automated checkout systems reduce labor time for routine transactions. Positive effect on workers: new roles in data analytics, logistics, and digital marketing have been created. Negative effect: cashier and in-store sales positions have been reduced or eliminated. Other industries and effects are valid.
questions.explanation: Technology creates disruption (job displacement) and opportunity (new roles and industries) simultaneously.

questions.question: You are launching a small business selling handmade phone cases at school. Define your target market, name one CSR practice you could adopt, and identify one ethical challenge you might face.
questions.answer: Target market: high school students aged 14–18 who want affordable, unique phone accessories. CSR practice: donating 5% of profits to a school club or using eco-friendly packaging materials. Ethical challenge: ensuring product claims (e.g., 'waterproof') are accurate; not using misleading advertising to attract buyers; fair pricing that does not exploit peers. Other valid answers accepted.
questions.explanation: This question integrates marketing (target market), CSR, and business ethics within a practical scenario.

questions.question: A food truck has fixed monthly costs of $1,800 and variable costs of $4 per meal. It sells each meal for $10. Calculate the break-even quantity and explain what happens to profit if the owner sells 100 meals beyond break-even.
questions.answer: Break-even quantity = Fixed Costs / (Price − Variable Cost) = $1,800 / ($10 − $4) = $1,800 / $6 = 300 meals. If the owner sells 100 meals beyond break-even (400 total), the additional profit is 100 × $6 = $600, because fixed costs are already covered and each extra meal contributes $6 in contribution margin.
questions.explanation: Break-even analysis: Fixed costs divided by contribution margin per unit. Every unit sold beyond break-even adds contribution margin directly to profit.

quizQuestions.question: Which type of business is easiest to set up but exposes the owner to unlimited personal liability?
quizQuestions.explanation: A sole proprietorship requires no formal registration in most jurisdictions but the owner is personally liable for all debts.

quizQuestions.question: A stakeholder is best defined as:
options: A shareholder who owns more than 50% of a company
Anyone who is affected by or has an interest in a business's decisions
Only the employees of a company
The government agency that regulates a company
quizQuestions.answer: Anyone who is affected by or has an interest in a business's decisions
quizQuestions.explanation: Stakeholders include employees, customers, suppliers, communities, investors, and governments — anyone with a stake in the business.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "Which type of business is easiest to set up but exposes the owner to unlimited personal liability" is: Sole proprietorship.
quizQuestions.explanation: True. Sole proprietorship is correct.

quizQuestions.question: Fill in the blank: Complete: In the context of the topic, ___ refers to "which of the following is a primary goal common to most businesses."
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Generating profit.

quizQuestions.question: Scarcity occurs because:
options: People do not want many things
Resources are limited while human wants are unlimited
quizQuestions.answer: Resources are limited while human wants are unlimited
quizQuestions.explanation: Scarcity is the core economic problem: society has finite resources but infinite desires.

quizQuestions.question: You choose to watch a movie instead of studying. The opportunity cost is:
options: The price of the movie ticket
The time you spent watching the movie
The knowledge and grade improvement from studying
The cost of your internet subscription
quizQuestions.answer: The knowledge and grade improvement from studying
quizQuestions.explanation: Opportunity cost is the value of the best alternative you gave up — in this case, studying and its academic benefits.

quizQuestions.question: Which of the following best describes the law of demand?
options: As price rises, quantity supplied rises
As price falls, quantity demanded falls
As price rises, quantity demanded falls
Supply and demand are always equal
quizQuestions.answer: As price rises, quantity demanded falls
quizQuestions.explanation: The law of demand: holding all else equal, higher prices lead to lower quantity demanded.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "Scarcity occurs because:" is: Resources are limited while human wants are unlimited.
quizQuestions.explanation: True. Resources are limited while human wants are unlimited is correct.

quizQuestions.question: Market equilibrium occurs when:
options: Quantity supplied is greater than quantity demanded
quizQuestions.explanation: At equilibrium the market clears — no surplus or shortage exists.

quizQuestions.question: If the price of a good is above equilibrium, which situation results?
quizQuestions.explanation: Above the equilibrium price, quantity supplied exceeds quantity demanded, creating a surplus.

quizQuestions.question: In a perfectly competitive market, individual firms are best described as:
quizQuestions.explanation: In perfect competition, many small firms sell identical products and must accept the market price — they are price takers.

quizQuestions.question: Which business function is responsible for converting inputs into finished goods or services?
quizQuestions.explanation: Operations management oversees the production process — transforming raw materials and labor into products or services.

quizQuestions.question: The finance department of a business is primarily responsible for:
quizQuestions.explanation: Finance handles financial planning, budgeting, accounting, cash management, and investment decisions.

quizQuestions.question: Marketing and operations must work together primarily because:
options: They share the same office space
Sales forecasts from marketing inform production planning in operations
Marketing designs the products that operations sells
quizQuestions.answer: Sales forecasts from marketing inform production planning in operations
quizQuestions.explanation: Marketing data on expected demand helps operations plan production capacity and inventory levels.

quizQuestions.question: HR (Human Resources) is responsible for:
options: Recruiting, training, and managing employee relations
quizQuestions.answer: Recruiting, training, and managing employee relations
quizQuestions.explanation: HR's core functions include talent acquisition, onboarding, training, performance management, and employee welfare.

quizQuestions.question: Which of the following best describes an entrepreneur?
options: A person who only invests money in existing companies
A person who organizes resources to create a new business and accepts risk for potential reward
A government official who regulates business
A senior manager at a large corporation
quizQuestions.answer: A person who organizes resources to create a new business and accepts risk for potential reward
quizQuestions.explanation: Entrepreneurs identify opportunities, gather resources, build businesses, and bear the financial and personal risk of failure.

quizQuestions.question: A market gap or opportunity exists when:
options: Consumer needs are not fully satisfied by existing products or services
A company has too much inventory
quizQuestions.answer: Consumer needs are not fully satisfied by existing products or services
quizQuestions.explanation: Entrepreneurs look for unmet or underserved needs in the market as the starting point for a new business.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "Which of the following best describes an entrepreneur" is: A person who organizes resources to create a new business and accepts risk for potential reward.
quizQuestions.explanation: True. A person who organizes resources to create a new business and accepts risk for potential reward is correct.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "A market gap or opportunity exists when:" is: The government removes business regulations.
quizQuestions.explanation: False. The correct answer is: Consumer needs are not fully satisfied by existing products or services.

quizQuestions.question: Which of the following is NOT a function of money?
quizQuestions.explanation: The three functions of money are medium of exchange, unit of account, and store of value. Comparative advantage is an economic trade concept unrelated to money's functions.

quizQuestions.question: Which type of account is best suited for everyday purchases and bill payments?
quizQuestions.explanation: Checking accounts offer easy access to funds through debit cards, checks, and electronic transfers, making them ideal for daily transactions.

quizQuestions.question: Interest is best defined as:
options: The fee a bank charges for opening an account
The cost of borrowing money or the return on deposited money
The government tax on financial transactions
The minimum balance required by a bank
quizQuestions.answer: The cost of borrowing money or the return on deposited money
quizQuestions.explanation: Interest is paid by borrowers to lenders (or by banks to depositors) as compensation for the use of money over time.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "Which of the following is NOT a function of money" is: Source of comparative advantage.
quizQuestions.explanation: True. Source of comparative advantage is correct.

quizQuestions.question: A budget is best described as:
options: A record of past spending only
A plan for how to allocate income across expenses and savings
A government document for public spending
A list of wants and needs
quizQuestions.answer: A plan for how to allocate income across expenses and savings
quizQuestions.explanation: A personal budget outlines expected income and planned expenses to guide financial decision-making.

quizQuestions.question: Which of the following is a NEED rather than a WANT?
quizQuestions.explanation: Housing is a basic need — essential for survival and functioning. The others are discretionary.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "A budget is best described as:" is: A plan for how to allocate income across expenses and savings.
quizQuestions.explanation: True. A plan for how to allocate income across expenses and savings is correct.

quizQuestions.question: Revenue is best defined as:
options: The profit left after all expenses
Total money earned from sales before any costs are deducted
The total of all fixed costs
quizQuestions.answer: Total money earned from sales before any costs are deducted
quizQuestions.explanation: Revenue (also called sales or turnover) is the gross income from business activities before subtracting costs.

quizQuestions.question: A cost that changes with the level of production is called:
quizQuestions.explanation: Variable costs (e.g., raw materials, packaging) increase as production increases and decrease when output falls.

quizQuestions.question: Break-even point is calculated as:
options: Fixed Costs / Contribution Margin per Unit
quizQuestions.answer: Fixed Costs / Contribution Margin per Unit
quizQuestions.explanation: Contribution margin = Price − Variable Cost per unit. Break-even = Fixed Costs ÷ Contribution Margin.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "Revenue is best defined as:" is: Total money earned from sales before any costs are deducted.
quizQuestions.explanation: True. Total money earned from sales before any costs are deducted is correct.
